subtopics.title: What's in the Box
subtopics.content:
#### Core Boards & Structures
- **1 game board** — Main play surface with locations and tracks.
- **1 Ever Tree** — Card stand and season worker staging structure.

#### Cards
- **128 Critter & Construction cards** — Main deck used for city building.
- **11 Forest cards** — Variable forest locations for setup.
- **16 Special Event cards** — Shuffle and reveal 4 each game.
- **1 Victory card** — Included game component.

#### Events & Markers
- **4 Basic Event tiles** — Always used in setup.
- **20 Occupied tokens** — Mark construction occupancy for free critter plays.
- **Point tokens** — 10 tokens worth 3 points and 20 tokens worth 1 point.

#### Resources
- **30 berries** — Resource type.
- **30 twigs** — Resource type.
- **25 resin** — Resource type.
- **20 pebbles** — Resource type.

#### Workers & Solo
- **24 workers** — 6 workers per player color for multiplayer flow.
- **1 eight-sided die** — Used for solo Rugwort system.

#### Supply Reminder
- **Resource cap** — There is no limit to resources a player may hold.
- **Substitution rule** — If a resource type runs out physically, use substitutes.

subtopics.title: Board Layout
subtopics.content:
#### Universal Setup Order
1. **Place board** — Set the game board at center table.
2. **Place Ever Tree** — Attach/place it on the board stump.
3. **Place resources** — Put twigs, resin, pebbles, and berries by the river bank.
4. **Place tokens** — Put point tokens and occupied tokens beside the board.
5. **Place events** — Put all 4 Basic Event tiles on the river spaces.
6. **Special events** — Shuffle Special Event cards and reveal 4 on lower Ever Tree branches.
7. **Meadow setup** — Shuffle main deck, reveal 8 face-up Meadow cards, and place deck in the Ever Tree.
8. **Discard zone** — Leave a clear face-down discard pile area near the deck.

#### Forest Card Setup by Player Count
--- 2 Players ---
- **Forest cards used** — Reveal 3 Forest cards on forest clearings.
- **Unused forest cards** — Return remaining Forest cards to the box.

--- 3 Players ---
- **Forest cards used** — Reveal 4 Forest cards on forest clearings.
- **Unused forest cards** — Return remaining Forest cards to the box.

--- 4 Players ---
- **Forest cards used** — Reveal 4 Forest cards on forest clearings.
- **Unused forest cards** — Return remaining Forest cards to the box.

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
#### Worker Allocation (All Multiplayer Counts)
- **Color choice** — Each player chooses one color.
- **Total workers** — Each player takes 6 workers of their color.
- **Starting workers** — Keep 2 workers available in your personal supply.
- **Tree staging** — Put remaining 4 workers on season branches: 1 on spring, 1 on summer, 2 on autumn.

#### Starting Hand by Seat Position
- **First player** — Draw 5 cards.
- **Second player** — Draw 6 cards.
- **Third player** — Draw 7 cards.
- **Fourth player** — Draw 8 cards.

#### Setup Clarifier
- **No starting resources** — Players gain resources through actions, not initial grants.

subtopics.title: Starting the Game
subtopics.content:
#### Player-Count Audit (Step 0 Reference)
- **2-player setup difference** — Use 3 Forest cards.
- **3-player setup difference** — Use 4 Forest cards.
- **4-player setup difference** — Use 4 Forest cards.
- **Seat-order hand difference** — Hand sizes are 5/6/7/8 by first/second/third/fourth player.
- **4-player rules difference** — Extra marked forest placement locations are used.
- **4-player forest restriction** — You still may not place 2 of your own workers on the same Forest location.
- **Solo mode present** — Solo rules use Rugwort and the 8-sided die (separate mode).

#### Multiplayer Start
- **First player rule** — The most humble player goes first.
- **Turn order** — Play proceeds clockwise.
- **Turn choices** — On your turn, take exactly one action: Place a Worker, Play a Card, or Prepare for Season.
- **Season timing gate** — Prepare for Season is only legal after all current workers are deployed.

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
#### Per-Turn Sequence
1. **Choose one action** — Place a Worker, Play a Card, or Prepare for Season.
2. **Resolve fully** — Complete the chosen action and all immediate effects.
3. **Handle Meadow refresh** — If Meadow cards were played/drawn by effect, follow draw-then-replenish timing.
4. **Enforce hand cap** — You may never exceed 8 cards in hand.
5. **Pass clockwise** — Next player takes their turn.

#### Turn-Flow Clarifiers
- **Independent pacing** — Players change seasons independently, not in synchronized rounds.
- **Action limit** — You take one action per turn, never multiple standard actions.

subtopics.title: Actions & Options
subtopics.content:
#### Action A: Place a Worker
- **Placement rule** — Place one available worker on a legal worker symbol.
- **Immediate effect** — Gain listed resources/effects right away.
- **Worker lock** — Worker stays deployed until your next Prepare for Season.

#### Location Types
- **Exclusive location** — Only one worker total may occupy it.
- **Shared location** — Multiple workers may occupy it, including same color.
- **Destination in your city** — Always legal for your workers.
- **Open destination in other city** — Legal only if card has the open-visit symbol; owner gains 1 point token.

#### Haven
- **Unlimited occupancy** — Any number of workers may use Haven.
- **Card conversion** — Discard any number of hand cards.
- **Rate** — Gain 1 resource of your choice per 2 discarded cards.

#### Events
- **Eligibility gate** — You must meet all listed requirements before placing.
- **Cost timing** — Pay event costs at the moment you place the worker.
- **Uniqueness** — Each event can be claimed by only one player.

#### Journey (Autumn Only)
- **Season lock** — Journey is only available in autumn.
- **Cost** — Discard cards equal to the selected journey point value.
- **Space type** — 5/4/3-point journey spaces are exclusive; 2-point journey is shared.

#### Action B: Play a Card
- **Card source** — Play from hand or from Meadow.
- **Cost payment** — Pay printed resource cost into general supply.
- **Card classes** — Cards are Critters or Constructions.
- **Common duplication** — You may play multiple copies of Common cards.
- **Unique restriction** — You may have only one copy of each specific Unique card in your city.

#### Free Critter via Linked Construction
- **Free-play condition** — If linked Construction is already in your city, you may play that Critter without berry cost.
- **Occupancy marker** — Place an occupied token on that Construction.
- **One-time use** — Each Construction can grant this free Critter play once.

#### City Capacity
- **City limit** — Max 15 cards in your city.
- **Event exemption** — Event cards do not count toward the 15-card limit.

#### Action C: Prepare for Season
- **Timing gate** — Legal only after placing all currently available workers.
- **Retrieve workers** — Take back all your deployed workers.

#### Season Effects
- **New worker** — Gain 1 worker from the Ever Tree.
- **Production trigger** — Activate all green Production cards in your city.

- **New worker** — Gain 1 worker from the Ever Tree.
- **Meadow draw** — Draw up to 2 Meadow cards.
- **No season production** — Summer does not trigger global green Production.

- **New workers** — Gain 2 workers from the Ever Tree.
- **Production trigger** — Activate all green Production cards in your city.

#### 4-Player Forest Placement Difference
--- 4 Players ---
- **Extra forest spots active** — Use the additional marked forest placement locations.
- **Self-stacking restriction** — You still may not place 2 of your own workers on the same Forest location.

subtopics.title: Special Mechanics
subtopics.content:
#### Card Type Timing
- **Tan Traveler** — Activates once immediately when played.
- **Green Production** — Activates when played, plus during spring and autumn Prepare for Season.
- **Red Destination** — Activates when a worker is placed on it.
- **Blue Governance** — Grants conditional bonuses/discounts after certain plays.
- **Purple Prosperity** — Adds base points plus bonus points at endgame.

#### Card Draw Rules
- **Default draw source** — Draw from deck unless text says Meadow.
- **Meadow timing** — If an effect draws from Meadow, draw all required cards first, then replenish Meadow.
- **Deck exhaustion** — If deck runs out, shuffle discard pile to form new deck.

#### Hand & Transfer Limits
- **Hand cap** — Hard limit of 8 cards in hand at all times.
- **Overdraw prevention** — If instructed to draw above 8, draw only up to 8.
- **Give-card overflow** — If cards must be given and all opponents are at hand limit, discard those cards.

#### Resource Handling
- **Resource storage** — No limit on resource quantity in personal supply.
- **Physical shortage fix** — Use substitute markers if a resource type is depleted.

#### Solo Mode Reference
--- 1 Player (Solo) ---
- **Opponent system** — Play against Rugwort using solo rules.
- **Die usage** — Use the included 8-sided die for Rugwort card selection and behavior timing.

subtopics.title: Endgame & Scoring
subtopics.content:
#### End Trigger
- **Pass condition** — You pass when you cannot or choose not to take more actions.
- **Pass lock** — A passed player cannot take further turns.

#### Passed-Player Interaction Rule
- **No incoming gains** — Passed players cannot receive cards or resources.
- **Discard fallback** — If an effect must give cards/resources and all valid recipients have passed, discard instead.

#### Game End
- **Continue play** — Remaining players keep taking turns until all players have passed.
- **Then score** — Final scoring happens only after every player has passed.

#### Final Scoring Sources
- **Base card points** — Score printed points on cards in your city.
- **Point tokens** — Add all 1-point and 3-point tokens gained during play.
- **Prosperity bonuses** — Add purple Prosperity endgame bonus points.
- **Journey points** — Add points from workers sent on journey spaces.
- **Event points** — Add points from achieved Basic and Special Events.

#### Winner & Tiebreakers
- **Winner** — Highest total points wins.
- **Tiebreaker 1** — Most Events achieved wins the tie.
- **Tiebreaker 2** — If still tied, most leftover resources wins.

subtopics.title: Opening Priorities
subtopics.content:
#### Early Turn Focus
- **Engine first** — Prioritize cards or locations that improve repeatable resource/card conversion.
- **Green timing** — Play at least one useful green Production card before spring/autumn triggers for extra value.
- **City-slot discipline** — Reserve early city spaces for cards that scale or unlock options, not filler.

#### Meadow Control
- **Shared market awareness** — Track Meadow every turn because all players can buy from it.
- **Deny key links** — Take Meadow cards that complete obvious opponent combos if affordable.

#### Season Tempo
- **Prepare proactively** — Advance season when your remaining placements are weak.
- **Avoid dead turns** — Do not stay in season just to place low-impact workers.

#### Player-Count-Aware Opening Notes
--- 2 Players ---
- **Forest scarcity** — With only 3 Forest cards, contested forest effects appear sooner.
- **Seat hand spread** — First player starts at 5 cards and second at 6; second can often pivot earlier.

--- 3 Players ---
- **Forest density** — 4 Forest cards increase tactical routing options.
- **Seat hand spread** — Hand sizes 5/6/7 create asymmetry; third player can leverage broader opening choices.

--- 4 Players ---
- **Forest crowding** — 4 Forest cards plus extra marked forest spots still produce high competition.
- **Seat hand spread** — Fourth player starts with 8 cards, so early card quality planning matters more than raw draw speed.

subtopics.title: Common Mistakes
subtopics.content:
- **Hand-cap misses** — Forgetting the 8-card limit and over-planning draws that cannot legally happen.
- **City-cap bloat** — Filling the 15-card city with low-impact cards before your scoring engine is online.
- **Late season shift** — Waiting too long to Prepare for Season and burning turns on weak placements.
- **Free-critter miss** — Paying berries for a Critter when linked Construction could have played it free.
- **Occupancy error** — Reusing the same Construction for multiple free Critter plays after it is occupied.
- **Event tunnel vision** — Chasing event requirements too late, then discovering your city cannot meet prerequisites in time.
- **Meadow neglect** — Treating Meadow as secondary and gifting opponents uncontested high-value picks.

subtopics.title: Key Decisions
subtopics.content:
#### Decision 1: Engine Card vs Immediate Points
- **Engine line** — Choose repeatable production/governance effects when they create multiple future gains.
- **Point line** — Take immediate VP cards when your economy can still support later requirements.

#### Decision 2: Season Timing
- **Advance early** — Gain workers/triggers sooner but may leave some current-season spaces unused.
- **Delay advance** — Extract more from current board but risk low-impact placements and tempo loss.

#### Decision 3: Meadow Race vs Hand Plan
- **Meadow race** — Deny opponents and react to visible opportunities, but may dilute your city focus.
- **Hand plan** — Build coherent private combo lines, but risk being outpaced on shared market value.

#### Decision 4: Event Route vs Broad Scoring
- **Event route** — Target specific prerequisites for efficient point bundles.
- **Broad route** — Keep flexible city composition to avoid clogging your 15-card limit with narrow enablers.

metadata.notes: Reformatted for scannable/skimmable style with explicit player-count audit and count-specific headers.
